qa_pairs.question: How do you create a private image from a volume using the OpenStack Command Line Interface (CLI) during a migration?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a private image from a volume using the OpenStack CLI, you can use the command `openstack image create –volume <volume name/id> <newimagename> --private`.

qa_pairs.question: How can you check the status of a newly created image using the OpenStack Command Line Interface (CLI), and what indicates its completion?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check the status of a newly created image by running the command `openstack image show <newimagename>`. The status will eventually change from 'saving' to 'active', which indicates the image creation is complete. This process may take an hour or longer, depending on the volume's size.
